samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size" value="4">Multiplier size parameter.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extends the multiplicand by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.</assign>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extends the multiplier by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_product[0]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the first partial product based on the least significant bit of the multiplier.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_product[1]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the second partial product based on the second bit of the multiplier.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_product[2]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the third partial product based on the third bit of the multiplier.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_product[3]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the fourth partial product based on the most significant bit of the multiplier.</assign>
        <assign id="add_reg[0]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the partial products.</assign>
        <assign id="add_reg[1]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the partial products.</assign>
        <assign id="add_reg[2]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the partial products.</assign>
        <assign id="add_reg[3]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the partial products.</assign>
        <assign id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the final product by summing up the register values.</assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Extending inputs
        assign mul_a_ext = {4'b0000, mul_a};
        assign mul_b_ext = {4'b0000, mul_b};
        
        // Generating partial products
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin : pp_gen
                assign partial_product[i] = (mul_b_ext[i]) ? (mul_a_ext << i) : 'd0;
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Registering partial products
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                add_reg[0] <= 8'd0;
                add_reg[1] <= 8'd0;
                add_reg[2] <= 8'd0;
                add_reg[3] <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                add_reg[0] <= partial_product[0];
                add_reg[1] <= add_reg[0] + partial_product[1];
                add_reg[2] <= add_reg[1] + partial_product[2];
                add_reg[3] <= add_reg[2] + partial_product[3];
            end
        end
        
        // Calculating final product
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                mul_out <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_out <= add_reg[3];
            end
        end
    </implementation>
    
endmodule
